items.text:
Campus Mailroom Holiday Hours
Effective: December 20 to January 2
Location: Student Union, Room 105

Please be advised that the campus mailroom will operate on a reduced schedule during the winter break. Regular semester hours (9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday-Friday) will resume on January 3.

Holiday Schedule:
• December 20-23: 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM
• December 24-27: CLOSED
• December 28-30: 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM
• December 31-January 2: CLOSED

Important Reminders:
• Package pick-up requires a valid student ID.
• A $5.00 late fee will be charged for packages held over 30 days.
• Mail forwarding must be requested online via the student portal by December 15.

For questions, contact mailroom@university.edu or call (555) 123-4567.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student do to pick up a package?

options.A: Pay a $5.00 late fee in advance.
options.B: Request mail forwarding by December 15.
options.C: Present a valid student identification card.
options.D: Visit the mailroom during regular semester hours.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Package pick-up requires a valid student ID.'

options.A: begin again
options.B: summarize
options.C: shorten
options.D: cancel

questions.explanation: In the context of hours restarting after a break, 'resume' means to begin again.

options.B: It will not be open for service.
options.C: It will operate from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM.
options.D: It will charge a late fee for package pick-up.

questions.explanation: The notice lists December 24-27 as CLOSED, so December 26 is included in that closure period.

items.text:
Laundry Room Renovation – Anderson Hall
Dates: March 12 – April 2, 2025

Please be informed that the laundry facilities in Anderson Hall basement will be temporarily closed for renovation starting Monday, March 12. The project is scheduled for completion by Wednesday, April 2. During this period, residents of Anderson Hall may use the laundry rooms in the following locations:
• Carter Hall (first floor): 6 washers, 8 dryers
• University Commons (lower level): 10 washers, 12 dryers

Key Information:
• Access to alternate locations requires your student ID card.
• Machine rates remain $2.00 per wash and $1.50 per dry cycle.
• The renovated Anderson Hall laundry room will feature new, energy-efficient machines.
• For maintenance requests at alternate sites, please contact Facilities at ext. 5555.

We appreciate your patience during these improvements.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the cost to dry one load of laundry during the renovation?

options.A: It requires a special access fee.
options.B: It is included with the wash cost.
options.C: It is one dollar and fifty cents.
options.D: It is two dollars per cycle.

questions.explanation: The notice states machine rates include '$1.50 per dry cycle.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the laundry facilities in Carter Hall?

options.A: They are located on the lower level.
options.B: They will also be renovated in April.
options.C: They have fewer dryers than washers.
options.D: They require a separate access fee.

questions.explanation: By comparing the listed machine counts for Carter Hall (6 washers, 8 dryers) and University Commons (10 washers, 12 dryers), one can infer they have different numbers of machines.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a price increase for laundry services.
options.B: To inform residents of a temporary closure and alternative options.
options.C: To describe new energy-efficient laundry machines.
options.D: To request that residents avoid using laundry facilities.

questions.explanation: The primary purpose is to announce the renovation closure (March 12-April 2) and provide alternative laundry locations.

items.text:
Temporary Closure of West Quad Laundry Room
Dates: October 15 – November 5

Notice is hereby given that the laundry room in West Quad Building D will be closed for essential plumbing repairs from Monday, October 15 through Tuesday, November 5. All other laundry rooms on campus will remain open during their regular hours.

Alternative Facilities:
• East Quad Laundry Room (Building A, Level 1): Open 7 AM–11 PM
• Student Center Laundry (Annex Room 12): Open 24 hours

Important Guidelines:
• Students must use their campus ID for access to alternative locations.
• A $10.00 refundable deposit is required to obtain a temporary access card for the Student Center laundry, if needed. Deposit refunds are processed at the Campus Services office.
• Report any issues with machines immediately to the Facilities hotline at 555-0100.

We apologize for the inconvenience. The upgraded system will improve service reliability.

format_metadata.title: Temporary Closure of West Quad Laundry Room

options.B: A report to the Facilities hotline.
options.C: Attendance during specific repair dates.
options.D: A valid campus identification card.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Students must use their campus ID for access to alternative locations,' which includes East Quad.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Student Center laundry facility?

options.A: It is the only alternative with 24-hour access.
options.B: It requires a reservation in advance.
options.C: It is located in Building D of West Quad.
options.D: It is closed during plumbing repairs.

questions.explanation: The notice lists Student Center Laundry as 'Open 24 hours,' while East Quad has hours '7 AM–11 PM.' Thus, it can be inferred the Student Center is the only listed alternative with 24-hour access.

options.A: To announce a new 24-hour laundry facility.
options.B: To notify residents of a closure and provide alternative arrangements.
options.C: To explain how to obtain a refund for laundry deposits.
options.D: To describe upcoming plumbing upgrades across campus.

items.text:
Subject: Action Required: Fall Semester Parking Permit Renewal

Dear Student,

This is a reminder that fall semester parking permits expire on August 31. To avoid a $45.00 late fee, you must renew your permit online through the Campus Portal by 5:00 PM on August 25.

Renewal Instructions:
1. Log in to the Campus Portal using your student credentials.
2. Navigate to the 'Vehicle Services' section.
3. Select 'Permit Renewal' and confirm your vehicle information.
4. Pay the $120.00 permit fee. Payment can be made by credit/debit card or electronic check.

Your new permit will be available for pick-up at the Parking Office (Smith Hall, Room 200) starting September 4. You'll need to show your student ID and vehicle registration. If you no longer need a parking permit, please update your status in the Portal to avoid further communications.

We're trying to streamline the process, so don't hesitate to contact us at parking@university.edu with questions.

Best regards,

Campus Parking Services

format_metadata.subject: Action Required: Fall Semester Parking Permit Renewal

options.A: Pick up the new permit by September 4.
options.B: Pay the permit fee with a credit card only.
options.C: Update their vehicle information in person.
options.D: Renew the permit online by August 25.

questions.explanation: The email states 'to avoid a $45.00 late fee, you must renew your permit online... by 5:00 PM on August 25.'

options.A: It is located in the Campus Portal.
options.B: It is closed for renovations in September.
options.C: It requires an appointment for permit pick-up.
options.D: It will not issue permits before September 4.

questions.explanation: The email states the new permit 'will be available for pick-up... starting September 4,' implying they will not be available before that date.

options.D: To explain how to update vehicle registration details.

questions.explanation: The email's primary purpose is to remind students of the expiration date and provide instructions and a deadline for renewal to avoid a late fee.

items.text:
Subject: Your Campus Parking Permit

Hi,

We've noticed your parking permit for Lot B is set to expire on May 15. You can renew it anytime between now and May 14 through the online portal. There's a $5.00 discount if you renew by May 1!

To complete your renewal:
• Log into the student portal and go to 'Campus Services.'
• Click 'Parking' and follow the prompts.
• The standard permit fee is $85.00 per semester.
• You'll receive a confirmation email with a digital permit you can print or display on your phone.

If you don't renew by May 15, your vehicle may be subject to ticketing. Also, remember that overnight parking rules are still enforced during the summer session. If you have any issues with the portal, just reply to this email or call us at (555) 789-0123.

Thanks,

Maria Chen
Parking Administration

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the standard fee for a semester parking permit?

options.A: Eighty-five dollars.
options.B: Five dollars with a discount.
options.C: One hundred twenty dollars.
options.D: Forty-five dollars after May 1.

questions.explanation: The email states 'The standard permit fee is $85.00 per semester.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the renewal process described in the email?

options.A: It must be completed in person at an office.
options.B: It offers a financial incentive for early action.
options.C: It is only available to students living on campus.
options.D: It requires a vehicle inspection first.

questions.explanation: The email mentions a '$5.00 discount if you renew by May 1,' which is a financial incentive for renewing early.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To warn a student about an upcoming parking ticket.
options.B: To introduce new overnight parking rules.
options.C: To provide technical support for the student portal.
options.D: To remind a permit holder about renewal options and deadlines.

questions.explanation: The email primarily serves as a reminder that the permit expires May 15, provides renewal instructions, mentions a discount, and warns of consequences for not renewing.

items.text:
BIOL 101: Introduction to Biology – Lab Component
Grading Breakdown:
• Lab Reports (10 total): 40% of final grade
• Midterm Practical Exam: 25%
• Final Practical Exam: 25%
• Participation & Attendance: 10%

Key Policies:
1. Lab reports are due at the start of the lab session one week after the experiment. Late reports will be penalized 10% per day, up to a maximum of 50%.
2. Attendance is mandatory. More than two unexcused absences will result in a full letter grade reduction.
3. Safety goggles and lab coats must be worn at all times in the laboratory. Failure to comply will result in dismissal from the session.

Important Dates:
• Midterm Practical: October 17, 2025 (during lab hours)
• Final Practical: December 12, 2025 (during lab hours)
• Last day to submit all lab reports: December 5, 2025

format_metadata.course_title: BIOL 101: Introduction to Biology – Lab Component

options.A: A 10% penalty on the final grade.
options.B: Automatic failure of the lab component.
options.C: A reduction of one full letter grade.
options.D: Required completion of extra lab reports.

questions.explanation: The syllabus states 'More than two unexcused absences will result in a full letter grade reduction.'

options.D: They are graded solely on participation.

questions.explanation: The syllabus lists 'Last day to submit all lab reports: December 5, 2025,' implying they will not be accepted after that date.

items.text:
@CampusDevTeam
Just launched the first version of 'CampusNav' – an app built by students for students! 🎉

It started as a class project last semester, but we kept working on it because we got so many requests for better campus maps. Now you can find the shortest route between classes, see real-time study room occupancy, and even get alerts for shuttle buses!

The best part? It's completely free and doesn't show ads. We're just passionate about making campus life easier. Give it a try and let us know what you think! Download links in our bio.

#CampusTech #StudentProject #UniversityLife

options.A: A need for real-time shuttle alerts.
options.B: Requests for a paid navigation service.
options.C: A project for an academic course.
options.D: A desire to display advertisements.

questions.explanation: The post states 'It started as a class project last semester.'

items.text:
Updated Lost and Found Policy – Campus Safety Office
Effective: January 16, 2025

To improve item recovery, the Campus Safety Office has updated its lost and found procedures. All found items should now be delivered directly to the Safety Office in Garner Hall, Room 101, during business hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday-Friday).

Key Changes:
• Items will be logged and stored for 90 days (previously 60 days).
• High-value items (electronics, wallets, keys) will be secured in locked storage.
• Claimants must provide a detailed description and show photo ID.
• A $2.00 administrative fee will be charged for reclaiming any item, payable at the time of pickup. This fee supports the maintenance of the lost and found system.

Unclaimed items will be donated or disposed of after the holding period. For questions, please call (555) 234-5678.

format_metadata.title: Updated Lost and Found Policy – Campus Safety Office

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the new holding period for lost items?

options.A: Thirty days from the date found.
options.B: Sixty days as per the previous policy.
options.C: During business hours only.
options.D: Ninety days after being logged.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Items will be logged and stored for 90 days (previously 60 days).'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $2.00 fee mentioned in the notice?

options.A: It is charged when reporting a lost item.
options.B: It is waived for high-value items like electronics.
options.C: It is a penalty for losing items frequently.
options.D: It is collected when a person retrieves their item.

questions.explanation: The notice states the fee is 'charged for reclaiming any item, payable at the time of pickup,' implying it is collected upon retrieval.

options.A: To inform the campus community of changes to lost and found procedures.

options.D: To list the business hours for Garner Hall.

questions.explanation: The notice announces 'updated its lost and found procedures' and details key changes like holding period, fee, and drop-off location.

items.text:
Community Garden Plot Registration

Registration for the Fall 2024 semester garden plots at the University Community Garden (located behind Anderson Hall) opens on September 12 at 9:00 AM. Plots are available on a first-come, first-served basis. All registered students must adhere to the following guidelines:

- Plot assignments will be finalized by September 19.
- A non-refundable $10 registration fee is required to secure your plot.
- All gardeners must attend a mandatory orientation on September 15 at 2:00 PM in Room 204 of the Student Union.
- Organic gardening practices are encouraged; chemical pesticides are prohibited.

Plots not maintained by October 15 may be reassigned. For questions, contact garden coordinator Ms. Chen at garden@university.edu or visit the Campus Life portal.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student do to secure a garden plot?

options.A: Attend a mandatory orientation session.
options.B: Submit a maintenance plan by October 15.
options.C: Pay a non-refundable ten-dollar fee.
options.D: Register online before September 12.

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'A non-refundable $10 registration fee is required to secure your plot.'

questions.stem: What will happen to a garden plot if it is not maintained by a specific date?

options.A: It will require an additional $5 penalty fee.
options.B: The gardener must attend a second orientation.
options.C: It may be given to another student.
options.D: Access to the garden will be permanently revoked.

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'Plots not maintained by October 15 may be reassigned.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the garden's operating policies?

options.A: They are designed to generate significant revenue for the university.
options.B: They allow students to use any gardening products they choose.
options.C: They prioritize early registration over previous gardening experience.
options.D: They emphasize environmental responsibility and student commitment.

questions.explanation: The notice encourages organic practices, prohibits chemicals, and requires a fee and orientation, implying an emphasis on environmental care and student follow-through.

items.text:
Updated Lost and Found Policy

Effective May 15, the Campus Security Office (Building 7, Room 112) is implementing a new lost and found policy to improve item recovery. The office's hours are Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. All items turned in will be logged and stored for 60 days. After this period, unclaimed items will be donated or disposed of.

Key changes include:
- A $2.00 processing fee is now required to claim any electronic device (laptops, tablets, phones).
- High-value items (wallets, jewelry, keys) must be claimed in person with valid student ID.
- Clothing and textbooks will be held for only 30 days.
- Found items can be reported online via the Campus Safety portal.

Please label your belongings. Contact security@university.edu with questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is a new requirement for claiming a lost laptop?

options.A: Providing a detailed description of the device.
options.B: Paying a two-dollar processing fee.
options.C: Submitting an online report within 24 hours.
options.D: Showing proof of purchase for the item.

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'A $2.00 processing fee is now required to claim any electronic device (laptops, tablets, phones).'

questions.stem: For how long will a lost textbook be stored before being disposed of?

options.A: For a period of 15 days.
options.B: Until the end of the current semester.
options.C: For a total of 30 days.
options.D: For a maximum of 60 days.

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'Clothing and textbooks will be held for only 30 days.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the purpose of the policy update?

options.A: To streamline operations and encourage students to claim items promptly.
options.B: To generate substantial revenue from students who lose items.
options.C: To reduce the number of items accepted by the security office.
options.D: To allow students to claim items outside of regular business hours.

questions.explanation: The policy sets specific holding periods and a small fee for electronics, which are measures likely intended to manage inventory efficiently and motivate timely retrieval.

items.text:
Subject: Apartment Maintenance Visit Scheduled

Hi Alex,

I just got a call from the building manager, Mr. Davies. The maintenance team needs to come by our apartment on Thursday, November 8, to inspect the heating system before winter. They'll be here between 1:00 PM and 4:00 PM.

We don't need to be home, but we should make sure the living room area is clear so they can access the vents. Could you please move any boxes or furniture away from the main vent by Wednesday night? I've already moved the couch.

Also, if you have a chance, can you pick up some filters for the HVAC unit? They're about $5 each at the hardware store. I'll Venmo you for them. Thanks!

Best,
Jamie

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Alex do before Wednesday night?

options.A: Schedule a meeting with the building manager.
options.B: Ensure the maintenance team has a key.
options.C: Purchase filters for the HVAC unit.
options.D: Clear items away from the living room vent.

questions.explanation: The email asks Alex, 'Could you please move any boxes or furniture away from the main vent by Wednesday night?'

options.B: clean.
options.C: reach.
options.D: install.

questions.explanation: In the context of making sure the living room is clear 'so they can access the vents,' 'access' means to be able to approach or reach them.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the relationship between Jamie and Alex?

options.A: They are professional colleagues working on a project.

questions.explanation: The email discusses their shared apartment, a maintenance visit, and tasks like moving furniture and buying filters, indicating a cooperative roommate dynamic.

items.text:
@CampusFoodie
Just checked out the weekly farmers market on University Ave – what a great find! They're there every Saturday from 8 AM to 1 PM. I got the most amazing fresh strawberries for only $4 a basket! The sourdough bread from 'Bread & Beyond' is also a must-try; it's $6 a loaf but totally worth it.

The vibe is so friendly, and lots of students were there. My tip: go early for the best selection. The apple cider sells out fast! I spent about $15 total and came home with a bag full of goodies. Definitely adding this to my weekend routine.

Has anyone else tried their honey? I'm thinking of getting some next week! #FarmersMarket #LocalEats #StudentLife

items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Campus Makerspace!

Dear New Member,

Welcome to the University Makerspace in Innovation Hall, Room 305! We're excited to have you. Your membership provides access to our 3D printers, laser cutters, and woodworking tools from September 1 to December 15.

Before your first visit, you must complete a brief online safety tutorial on our portal. It'll take about 20 minutes. Once done, you can book equipment online. Remember, the Makerspace is open Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM, and Saturdays from 12:00 PM to 4:00 PM.

We're hosting an open house on September 5 at 3:00 PM – it's a great chance to tour the space and meet our staff. We can't wait to see what you create!

Best regards,
The Makerspace Team
makerspace@university.edu

questions.stem: According to the email, what must a new member do before using the Makerspace equipment?

options.B: Schedule an appointment with a staff member.

options.D: Pay a membership fee for the semester.

questions.explanation: The email states, 'Before your first visit, you must complete a brief online safety tutorial on our portal.'

questions.explanation: In the context of 'Your membership provides access to our 3D printers...', 'access' means the ability to use or utilize the equipment.

options.D: It is closed for maintenance every Friday afternoon.

questions.explanation: The email lists hours for Monday to Friday and also for Saturdays, indicating it is open on both weekdays and a weekend day.

items.text:
@GreenThumbSam
Review of the Oak Street Community Garden Plant Sale!

This sale happens every spring and fall – the fall one was yesterday. I picked up two beautiful lavender plants for my balcony for just $8 each! They also had pots, soil, and gardening tools. The volunteers were super knowledgeable and helped me pick plants that do well in partial sun.

The only downside was how crowded it got by 11 AM. I'd recommend getting there right at the 9 AM opening next time. They take cash and card, which is convenient. I spent about $25 total.

Has anyone else been? What did you buy? I'm already planning for the spring sale! #CommunityGarden #Plants #CampusLife

questions.stem: According to the post, how much did the writer pay for each lavender plant?

options.A: Eight dollars per plant.
options.B: Five dollars for two plants.
options.C: Ten dollars for a large pot.
options.D: Twenty-five dollars in total.

questions.explanation: The post states, 'I picked up two beautiful lavender plants for my balcony for just $8 each!'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the writer's recommendation for attending the sale?

options.A: The best selection is available at the end of the day.
options.B: The payment process is faster if you bring exact cash.
options.C: The volunteers are most helpful in the afternoon.
options.D: Arriving early helps avoid large crowds.

questions.explanation: The writer mentions the sale got crowded by 11 AM and recommends 'getting there right at the 9 AM opening next time,' implying earlier arrival avoids crowds.

options.D: To provide detailed instructions for planting lavender.

questions.explanation: The post recounts the writer's visit, gives prices and a tip, and ends with a question to followers, making its primary purpose to share a personal review and recommendation.

items.text:
The Rolling Bean Campus Food Truck – Weekly Specials (October 21-27)

Location: Outside the Library Plaza
Hours: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM

**Hot Drinks**
- Fresh Brewed Coffee: $2.00
- Cafe Latte: $3.50
- Hot Chocolate: $2.75

**Lunch Specials** (Served 11:30 AM – 1:30 PM)
- 'Harvest Bowl' (quinoa, roasted veggies, tahini): $8.50
- 'Spicy Chicken Wrap' with side salad: $7.00
- Daily Soup (check board): $4.00

**Deals**
- Combo: Any wrap + medium drink: $9.00 (save $1.50!)
- Student Discount: Show ID for 10% off total order (excludes combos).
- We accept campus meal cards, credit/debit, and cash. A $0.50 fee applies for transactions under $5.00 if paying by card.

items.text:
Subject: Invitation to Film Society Screening & Discussion

Dear Film Society Members,

You are invited to our first event of the semester: a screening of the classic film 'Metropolis' followed by a faculty-led discussion. The event will be held on Friday, March 22, in Davis Auditorium (Room 101). Doors open at 6:30 PM, and the film starts promptly at 7:00 PM.

Admission is free for all current society members. Please RSVP by March 18 via the link on our club portal so we can arrange enough seating. Light refreshments will be provided. We encourage you to bring a friend – non-member guests are welcome for a $5 entry fee.

We look forward to seeing you there for an evening of film and conversation!

Sincerely,
Priya Sharma
President, University Film Society

items.text:
Study Room Reservation System Update
Effective Date: September 12

To improve access and fairness, the campus library is updating its study room reservation policies starting September 12. Reservations can be made online via the student portal up to seven days in advance. Rooms are available from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily.

Key Changes:
- A $5.00 late cancellation fee will be charged if a reservation is canceled less than 24 hours before the scheduled time.
- Groups must have a minimum of two students to book a room.
- All reservations are held for 15 minutes past the start time before being released.

For questions, contact the Library Help Desk at library@university.edu or visit Room 204 in Anderson Hall.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student do to avoid a $5.00 fee?

options.A: To book a room at least seven days in advance.
options.B: To have a group of at least two students.
options.C: To arrive within 15 minutes of the start time.
options.D: To cancel a reservation more than 24 hours ahead.

questions.explanation: The notice states a $5.00 fee is charged for cancellations made less than 24 hours before the scheduled time, so avoiding it requires canceling more than 24 hours ahead.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the new reservation system?

options.A: It is designed to reduce the number of available rooms.
options.B: It will be managed exclusively by library staff in Anderson Hall.
options.C: It allows individual students to book rooms for unlimited hours.
options.D: It aims to prevent rooms from being reserved but not used.

questions.explanation: The policies like the late cancellation fee and the 15-minute hold rule imply an intent to discourage no-shows and ensure rooms are used.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce new rules for using library study rooms.
options.B: To promote the online student portal for all campus services.
options.C: To explain the operating hours of Anderson Hall.
options.D: To introduce a new contact for library maintenance issues.

questions.explanation: The notice primarily introduces updated reservation policies, including fees, group rules, and hold times, for library study rooms.

items.text:
Mandatory Fire Drill – Science Building
Date: October 18
Time: 10:30 AM

A mandatory fire drill for all occupants of the Science Building (Rooms 100-250) will be conducted on October 18 at 10:30 AM. The drill is required by campus safety regulations.

Evacuation Procedures:
- Upon hearing the alarm, proceed immediately to the nearest marked exit.
- Do not use elevators. Assemble at the designated gathering area on the north lawn.
- Remain outside until an all-clear signal is given by safety personnel.

Important Notes:
- Classes in session at 10:30 AM will be paused for the drill.
- Failure to participate may result in a $10.00 safety compliance fee.
- For questions, contact Campus Safety at safety@university.edu or extension 5555.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where should people assemble after evacuating?

options.A: In the parking lot behind the building.
options.B: At the main entrance of the Science Building.
options.C: On the north lawn of the campus.
options.D: Inside Rooms 100-250 of the building.

questions.explanation: The notice explicitly states to assemble at the designated gathering area on the north lawn.

questions.stem: What is a potential consequence for not participating in the drill?

options.A: A meeting with the building manager.
options.B: A $10.00 safety compliance fee.
options.C: Loss of access to the Science Building.
options.D: Automatic failure of any class in session.

questions.explanation: The notice states that failure to participate may result in a $10.00 safety compliance fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about classes during the fire drill?

options.A: They will be canceled for the entire day.
options.B: Students must get permission to leave them.
options.C: Professors are required to record attendance.
options.D: They will be temporarily interrupted.

questions.explanation: The notice says 'Classes in session at 10:30 AM will be paused for the drill,' implying a temporary interruption.

Hi Jordan,

This email confirms your tutoring appointment at the Academic Success Center.

- Tutor: Ms. Lin
- Date: November 5

Please bring your student ID, textbook, and any specific questions. If you need to cancel or reschedule, you must do so at least 12 hours in advance through the online portal. Late cancellations prevent other students from booking time.

items.text:
Campus Shuttle Service – Winter Semester Timetable
Effective: January 10 – April 28

Route A: Main Campus Loop
- Monday to Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM (every 15 minutes)
- Saturday & Sunday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM (every 30 minutes)
- Stops: Library, Student Union, Science Quad, North Dorms

Route B: Downtown Express
- Weekdays only: 8:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 5:00 PM, 8:00 PM
- Stops: University Gate, City Center, Transit Hub

Policies:
- Service is free for all students with a valid ID.
- Bikes are allowed on exterior racks only.
- Shuttles depart promptly; please arrive 5 minutes early.
- For real-time tracking, use the 'Campus Transit' app.

Note: No service on university holidays (February 20, March 15).

format_metadata.title: Campus Shuttle Service – Winter Semester Timetable

options.B: Only on weekdays at specific times.

options.D: On weekdays and Saturdays every 30 minutes.

questions.explanation: The schedule states 'Route B: Downtown Express' runs on 'Weekdays only' at listed times.

options.D: The Main Campus Loop does not stop at the dorms.

questions.explanation: The note about 'No service on university holidays' implies riders need to plan for those specific date disruptions.

options.A: To inform students of shuttle routes, times, and rules for the semester.

options.C: To announce a new shuttle route connecting to the downtown area.
options.D: To list all university holidays when buildings will be closed.

items.text:
Annual Spring Fire Safety Drill – Residence Halls
Dates: April 10 & April 11
Time: Between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM each day

All students living in campus residence halls (Oak, Maple, Pine) must participate in the annual spring fire drill. Drills will occur randomly on either April 10 or April 11 between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM.

Procedures:
- Evacuate immediately via the nearest stairwell. Do not use elevators.
- Assemble at your hall's designated outdoor assembly point.
- Wait for instructions from your Resident Advisor (RA) or safety staff.

Compliance is mandatory. Students found in their rooms during the drill will be subject to a $15.00 fine and a meeting with Housing staff. Your cooperation ensures campus safety.

Questions? Contact Housing Office at housing@university.edu or call 555-2000.

format_metadata.title: Annual Spring Fire Safety Drill – Residence Halls

options.D: Loss of elevator access for the semester.

options.D: They require students to stay in the assembly area until 4:00 PM.

options.D: To schedule individual meetings between students and RAs.

questions.explanation: The notice's primary function is to communicate the mandatory drill schedule, procedures, and consequences for non-compliance.

items.text:
WATER SHUTOFF NOTICE - ANDERSON HALL
Date: May 15, 2024

Planned maintenance on the main water line will require a temporary shutoff for Anderson Hall and the adjacent Science Library. The shutoff is scheduled for Wednesday, May 15, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.

During this period:
• All restrooms and water fountains in Anderson Hall will be unavailable.
• The Science Library will remain open, but its restrooms will be closed.
• Portable restroom facilities will be available outside the main entrance.
• A $5.00 inconvenience fee credit will be applied to the campus dining accounts of all Anderson Hall residents.

For urgent issues, contact Facilities Management at ext. 5555. We apologize for the disruption.

items.text:
ELEVATOR MAINTENANCE SCHEDULE - MAPLE RESIDENCE HALL

Please be advised that scheduled maintenance will be performed on the elevators in Maple Residence Hall. The work is necessary for safety certification and will proceed as follows:

• Elevator A (North): Out of service Monday, September 12, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM.
• Elevator B (South): Out of service Monday, September 12, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM.

During these times, please use the stairwells. Residents with mobility accommodations who require assistance should contact the Residence Life Office at least 24 hours in advance at RL-office@campus.edu or ext. 3301. We appreciate your patience. A reminder email will be sent on September 10.

items.text:
IMPORTANT: WI-FI SECURITY CERTIFICATE UPDATE
To all students, faculty, and staff,

The campus Wi-Fi network (Eduroam-Campus) will undergo a mandatory security certificate update on Friday, March 8. Beginning at 6:00 AM, all devices will be disconnected and must accept the new certificate to reconnect.

Action Required:
• After 6:00 AM on March 8, manually select 'Eduroam-Campus' on your device.
• A pop-up will prompt you to accept the new security certificate. You must select 'Trust' or 'Accept'.
• Devices that do not accept the new certificate will be unable to connect.

Failure to update may result in a $15 fee for in-person technical support at the IT Help Desk in the University Center, Room 204. For automated instructions, text 'WIFI' to 55555. Contact IT Support at support@university.edu with questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what could result in a $15 fee?

options.A: Using the Wi-Fi before 6:00 AM on March 8.
options.B: Needing in-person help to reconnect.
options.C: Texting for automated instructions.
options.D: Contacting IT Support by email.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Failure to update may result in a $15 fee for in-person technical support at the IT Help Desk.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the certificate update process?

options.A: It will be performed automatically for all devices.
options.B: It is an optional security enhancement.
options.C: It requires users to take a specific action on their devices.
options.D: It will only affect students living on campus.

questions.explanation: The notice says users must manually select the network and accept a pop-up prompt, indicating active user action is required.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To introduce a new Wi-Fi network name.
options.B: To warn users about a potential virus.
options.C: To charge users for IT support services.
options.D: To explain a required network change and its consequences.

questions.explanation: The notice's primary goal is to inform the campus about a mandatory security update, the actions required, and the potential fee for non-compliance.

items.text:
Subject: Join the Airwaves! WCRU Radio Volunteer Recruitment

Dear Students,

Are you passionate about music, news, or sports? WCRU Campus Radio is looking for enthusiastic volunteers for the Fall semester! We've got openings for on-air DJs, news reporters, sports commentators, and behind-the-scenes production assistants. No prior experience is needed – we'll provide all the training.

Our first training session is on Thursday, August 25, at 7:00 PM in the Student Union Media Suite (Room 112). You'll learn about broadcasting equipment, FCC regulations, and content creation. It's a great way to build your resume, meet new people, and have a voice on campus.

If you're interested, please RSVP by August 20 by filling out the short form on our campus portal under 'Student Clubs.' We can't wait to hear from you!

Best regards,

Jamie Chen
Station Manager, WCRU Radio

questions.explanation: The email instructs interested students to 'RSVP by August 20 by filling out the short form on our campus portal.'

items.text:
Subject: Shift Swap Request for Friday

Hi Morgan,

I hope you're having a good week. I'm writing to see if you'd be willing to swap shifts with me at the Campus Bookstore this Friday, March 3. I'm scheduled for the 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM shift, but I have a dentist appointment I can't reschedule.

I can take your shift on Sunday, March 5, from 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM. I know the manager, Ms. Gupta, needs at least 48 hours' notice for any swaps to avoid the $10 late-change fee. If you agree, we both need to email her by Wednesday at 5:00 PM to get it approved.

Please let me know by tomorrow evening if that works for you. Thanks so much for considering it!

Best,
Alex

questions.stem: According to the email, what shift is Alex originally scheduled to work?

options.A: Sunday, March 5, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM.
options.B: Friday, March 3, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM.
options.C: Wednesday at 5:00 PM.
options.D: Friday, March 3, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM.

questions.explanation: Alex states 'I'm scheduled for the 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM shift' on Friday, March 3.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the bookstore's shift swap policy?

options.A: Swaps are not permitted for any reason.
options.B: The manager must approve swaps in advance.
options.C: Swaps are free if done on the same day.
options.D: Only full-time employees can swap shifts.

questions.explanation: Alex says they need to email the manager for approval and mentions a fee for late notice, implying manager approval is required.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of Alex's email?

options.A: To complain about the bookstore's scheduling system.
options.B: To request a trade of work shifts with a coworker.
options.C: To inform Morgan about a new store policy.
options.D: To confirm a meeting time with the manager.

questions.explanation: The email's subject and content are focused on asking Morgan to swap a specific shift.

items.text:
@DevStudentTaylor
After months of coding, my team's app 'CampusNav' is finally live on the student app store! It started as a project for Hackathon 2023, but we kept working on it. It gives you real-time locations for:
- Available study rooms in the library
- The shortest line at dining halls
- The next campus shuttle (with GPS tracking!)

The coolest feature? You can report issues like a flickering light in a hallway, and it goes directly to Facilities. We built it because we got lost looking for a study spot so many times. Check it out and let me know what you think! #StudentMade #AppDev #CampusTech #ProblemSolved

options.C: Available library study rooms.
options.D: Upcoming campus concerts.

questions.explanation: Taylor lists features including 'Available study rooms in the library.'

options.A: To promote a student-developed application.

options.D: To give a tutorial on how to build an app.

items.text:
Subject: Spring Semester Dorm Checkout Procedures

Dear Residents of Anderson Hall,

The semester ends on May 15, and all residents must check out by 12:00 PM (noon) on that day. Please follow these steps to avoid a $25 improper checkout fee.

1. Schedule a checkout inspection with your RA (Resident Advisor) by May 10 via the housing portal. Inspections run from May 12-14, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.
2. Remove all personal belongings and trash. A $10 cleaning fee will be charged for any items left behind.
3. Return your room key and mailbox key to the front desk. A $45 replacement fee applies for lost keys.

Your room must be clean and empty for the inspection. You'll receive your $200 security deposit refund within 30 days after a successful checkout.

Best regards,

Jamie Chen
Head Resident Advisor, Anderson Hall

questions.stem: According to the email, what must a resident do by May 10?

options.A: To schedule a checkout inspection with their RA.
options.B: To remove all personal belongings from their room.
options.C: To return their room and mailbox keys.
options.D: To receive their security deposit refund.

questions.explanation: The email states residents must 'Schedule a checkout inspection with your RA (Resident Advisor) by May 10 via the housing portal.'

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To outline the procedures and deadlines for leaving the dormitory.
options.B: To announce an increase in security deposit amounts for next year.
options.C: To introduce the new Head Resident Advisor for Anderson Hall.
options.D: To advertise summer housing options on campus.

questions.explanation: The email primarily provides detailed instructions, dates, and fees related to the end-of-semester dorm checkout process.

items.text:
@CraftyCampusChris
Just finished my final project for Sculpture 101 – a lamp made from recycled textbooks! The base is an old calculus book (sorry, Professor Lee!), and the shade is woven from pages of a worn-out campus map. It was tricky getting the wiring right, but the campus makerspace had all the tools I needed.

It's not perfect, but I love how it turned out! It really lights up my dorm desk. Now I just need to survive the critique on Friday. Wish me luck!

Has anyone else done a cool DIY project this semester? Share your pics below! #UpcycleChallenge #CampusDIY #FinalsWeekCreativity

questions.stem: According to the post, what did the writer use to make the lampshade?

options.A: Pages from a calculus textbook.
options.B: Pages from a worn-out campus map.
options.C: Tools from a personal workshop.
options.D: Wiring from an old appliance.

questions.explanation: The post states 'the shade is woven from pages of a worn-out campus map.'

questions.stem: The word 'critique' in the post is closest in meaning to

options.A: A celebration.
options.B: A formal evaluation.
options.C: A repair session.
options.D: A public sale.

questions.explanation: In the context of a final project for a sculpture class, a 'critique' refers to a session where the work is formally evaluated and discussed.

options.A: It is only open to engineering students.
options.B: It provides specialized tools for student projects.
options.C: It sells recycled materials like old books.
options.D: It is where the sculpture class is held.

questions.explanation: The writer mentions the makerspace had 'all the tools I needed' to complete the wiring for the lamp, implying it provides access to specialized equipment for student projects.

items.text:
NOTICE: Updated Lost & Found Policy
Effective: September 12
Location: Campus Safety Office, Room 114, Main Building
Hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM

To improve item recovery, the Campus Safety Office is implementing a new lost and found procedure.

• All items found in public areas (libraries, cafeterias, quads) must be brought to the Campus Safety Office within 24 hours.
• Valuable items (wallets, phones, laptops, keys) will be logged and stored securely for 60 days.
• Clothing, books, and water bottles will be held for 30 days.
• After the holding period, unclaimed items will be donated or discarded.

To claim an item:
1. Present a valid student or photo ID.
2. Provide a detailed description of the lost item.
3. For high-value electronics, you may be asked to demonstrate ownership (e.g., unlock the device).

A $2.00 administrative fee is required to claim any non-clothing item. Contact safety@university.edu or call (555) 123-4567 for inquiries.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the holding period for a lost textbook?

options.A: It will be held for 24 hours.
options.B: It will be held for 30 days.
options.C: It will be held for 60 days.
options.D: It will be held indefinitely.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Clothing, books, and water bottles will be held for 30 days.'

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce new hours for the Main Building.
options.B: To advertise a sale of unclaimed lost items.
options.C: To request donations of clothing and books.
options.D: To inform the community of revised procedures for lost items.

questions.explanation: The notice introduces a 'new lost and found procedure' and details the rules for submitting, storing, and claiming items.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about claiming a lost laptop?

options.A: It requires paying a fee and showing you can use it.
options.B: It is only possible on Mondays and Fridays.
options.C: It must be done within 24 hours of it being found.
options.D: It is free if claimed within the first week.

questions.explanation: The notice states a $2 fee for non-clothing items and, for high-value electronics, you may need to demonstrate ownership (e.g., unlock it). A laptop is both a non-clothing item and a high-value electronic.

Extended Hours & Finals Week Specials: December 10-18

All-Day Breakfast (Served 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM)

   (Cream cheese, butter, or peanut butter included)
• Yogurt Parfait with Granola & Fruit: $4.75

Study Fuel Combos (Available after 5:00 PM)
Combo includes any medium drink.
• Combo A: Grilled Cheese & Tomato Soup - $7.00
• Combo B: Personal Cheese Pizza - $8.50
• Combo B: Chicken Caesar Wrap - $8.00

Beverages
• Bottomless Coffee/Tea: $2.50
• Fresh Smoothies (Strawberry or Mango): $4.00

Payment & Policies
• Cash, credit, and campus meal cards accepted.
• No outside food or drink after 7:00 PM.
• Present your student ID for a 10% discount on all combos!

items.text:
Subject: Free Bicycle Maintenance Clinic – October 22

Hi Cyclists,

The Campus Sustainability Club is hosting a free Bicycle Maintenance Clinic on Saturday, October 22, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM in the parking lot behind the Recreation Center (Lot C).

Volunteer mechanics from "Gears & Years," a local bike shop, will be on hand to help you learn basic repairs. We'll cover:
• Fixing a flat tire
• Adjusting brakes and gears
• Proper chain cleaning and lubrication
• Basic safety checks

You should bring your own bicycle. We'll provide the tools and supplies, but if you have a specific issue, bring any spare parts you might have (like an inner tube). The first 30 participants will receive a free bike multi-tool (a $15 value)!

This is a great chance to get your bike ready for the fall semester and avoid repair costs. No registration is required—just show up! Email sustainability@campus.edu with questions.

Ride safe,

Alex Rivera
President, Campus Sustainability Club

questions.stem: According to the email, what will the clinic provide for participants?

options.A: Spare bicycle parts like inner tubes.
options.B: Registration for future paid workshops.
options.C: A discount at the "Gears & Years" bike shop.
options.D: The tools and supplies needed for repairs.

questions.explanation: The email states 'We'll provide the tools and supplies' for the maintenance clinic.

options.A: It is only available to club members.
options.B: It will be given to participants on a first-come basis.
options.C: It must be reserved online before the clinic.
options.D: It is the main topic of the repair lessons.

questions.explanation: The email states 'The first 30 participants will receive a free bike multi-tool,' implying a limited quantity available to those who arrive early.

options.A: To advertise a new local bicycle shop on campus.
options.B: To recruit volunteers for the Sustainability Club.

options.D: To sell multi-tools and other bicycle accessories.

questions.explanation: The email's primary function is to inform cyclists about a clinic where they can learn basic repair skills, detailing the time, place, and activities.
